narrative.challenge: The Customer Directory was built for credit unions, healthcare networks, and K–12 institutions — letting these organisations manage contact lists, build segments, and trigger targeted surveys. I owned all design decisions end-to-end: information architecture, interaction design, usability research, stakeholder alignment, and a 47-component design system.
narrative.solution: The project's defining challenge wasn't UI complexity. It was identifying who we were actually designing for — before we shipped the wrong answer at scale.

title: Scrapping $180K of engineering work — and shipping the product that was actually needed
description: Six weeks into development, I discovered we were building for the wrong users. Evidence convinced leadership to pivot. The redesigned product shipped within three months.
ndaNote: Employer and product details anonymised at their request. Full context available under mutual NDA.
